injected: Two independent changes, both required together to explain the failure: (1) pipeline/extract.py truncates every order_date to Jan 1 of its year via .dt.to_period("Y").dt.to_timestamp() before transform ever sees it; (2) pipeline/transform.py buckets with .dt.strftime("%Y") instead of "%Y-%m". Fixing either one alone still fails -- extract.py already destroyed the month info, or transform.py still collapses whatever date it's given.
intended_fix: Fix both: in extract.py, drop the .dt.to_period("Y").dt.to_timestamp() truncation and keep df["order_date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["order_date"]); in transform.py, change the strftime format back to "%Y-%m".
symptom_fix_to_watch_for: Fixing only transform.py's format string looks like progress -- order_month values change shape (e.g. all become "2025-01") -- but test_output_grain still fails, because extract.py already collapsed every date to Jan 1 of its year; there's no month information left to format correctly. Don't stop after the first fix just because the output 'looks' different; both files have to change before row count reaches 112.
